reviews.review_msg: Overall a positive experience. The professor explains things well, even if the pacing isn't always perfect. Assignments are fair and help reinforce the concepts.

I found this course to be interesting and useful overall. The lectures are insightful, and the professor tries to connect the material to real business situations. Participation is important, but it helps you stay involved. Definitely learned a lot from this class.

Honestly, I had a good experience in this class. The professor explains concepts clearly and uses real-world examples that make things easier to understand. If you stay engaged and keep up with the work, you can learn a lot. He's also pretty approachable if you have questions.
Criteria is extremely vague and due dates are got moved. Lecture is extremely long when the content itself can be summed into 10 minutes for the entire class.
The class is nice, and I learned a lot. The assignments are interesting if you spend some time on them. He is flexible with students situations and always tries to find a way to satisfy the class.

This professor does not know how to teach. He provides no criteria for his "assignments" that were originally given as participation activities (not for marks). He once made the class do 25 min activity at 5:15 pm... would not even bother taking any classes with him.
Great teacher, he explains very well a lot of concepts found in other courses that you didn't get to understand.
The lectures are quite insightful, relevant, and up-to-date.

The course felt disorganized, especially early on. Ex: spent 40 minutes on something that could have been done on canvas in 5. Lectures are fine, but not very engaging. End-of-class assignments are often vague and difficult to complete on time due to unclear expectations. Avoid if possible
He is a sessional instructor who does not know how to teach, really not recommended...
